title: Salmon Spread Appetizers

Main: 5.3 oz (1 container) Boursin garlic & fine herbs spreadable cheese
5 oz tin lightly smoked salmon (such as Trader Joe’s), drained of excess oil
2 tsp fresh dill, finely chopped (plus extra sprigs for garnish)
1 tsp capers, drained and roughly chopped
½ small shallot, very finely minced (optional, for extra depth)
Zest of ½ orange and ½ lemon (optional, adds citrus brightness)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
12‑15 orange mini sweet peppers, halved lengthwise and seeds removed

steps: Make the spread: In a medium bowl, combine the Boursin cheese, smoked salmon, dill, capers, shallot (if using), and citrus zest. Mix until smooth and evenly incorporated. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper; taste and adjust.
Prepare the peppers: Rinse the orange mini peppers, slice each in half lengthwise, and gently remove the seeds and membranes.
Stuff: Spoon or pipe the salmon spread into each pepper half, filling generously but not over‑packing.

Serve: Arrange the stuffed peppers on a platter. Garnish with a small dill sprig and, if desired, a tiny grating of orange or lemon zest. Serve immediately, or chill for up to 2 hours before serving for a firmer texture.
